metadata.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: A fact table that contains DEX swap activity for the following protocols: animeswap, auxexchange, batswap, cellana, cetus, hippo, liquidswap, pancake, sushi, thala, and tsunami. This fact table only contains the raw on chain data with no decimal adjustments, token symbols, or prices appiled.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: Unique identifier for the event. This is a monotonically increasing integer that is incremented for each event. This is useful for determining the order of changes.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The name of the platform where the swap occurred.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The full address of the inbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ
AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.comment: The non decimal adjusted amount of a token of the inbound token for the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.comment: The non decimal adjusted amount of a token of the outbound token for the swap.

FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID
FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

metadata.name: FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates bridge activity for bridge deposits and transfers sent from the following protocols: CELER, LAYER ZERO, MOVER, and WORMHOLE. Note, this table only includes records for the protocols listed above with live, onchain bridge activity and may not represent the complete bridging picture.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The platform or protocol from which the bridge transaction or event originates.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract responsible for handling the bridge deposit or transfer. This contract mediates the transfer and ensures that assets are sent and received appropriately.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The third segment of the event type

TX_SENDER.name: TX_SENDER
TX_SENDER.comment: Sender is the address of the originator account for a transaction. A transaction must be signed by the originator.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The address that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer. This address is the sender of the tokens/assets being bridged to the destination chain.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the bridged tokens on the target chain after the completion of the bridge transaction. For non-evm chains, the hex address is decoded/encoded to match the data format of the destination chain, where possible.

SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.name: SOURCE_CHAIN_ID
SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the source blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

SOURCE_CHAIN_NAME.name: SOURCE_CHAIN_NAME
SOURCE_CHAIN_NAME.comment: The name of the blockchain network from which the assets are being bridged.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the destination blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_NAME.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_NAME
DESTINATION_CHAIN_NAME.comment: The name of the blockchain network to which the assets are being bridged.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address associated with the token that is being bridged. It provides a unique identifier for the token within its origin blockchain.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of a token. For example, if a token has 18 decimals, then the amount of 1 token is 10^18.

FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_NFT_MINTS
metadata.comment: A convenience model that combines fact nft mints with tokens and prices

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp at which the NFT event occurred.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number at which the NFT event occurred.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the NFT event. This is not necessarily unique in this table as a transaction may contain multiple NFT events.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The event number within a transaction

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of NFT event in this transaction, either sale, bid_won or redeem.

NFT_FROM_ADDRESS.name: NFT_FROM_ADDRESS
NFT_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of the NFT in the transaction.

NFT_TO_ADDRESS.name: NFT_TO_ADDRESS
NFT_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of the NFT in the transaction.

NFT_ADDRESS.name: NFT_ADDRESS
NFT_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address of the NFT.

TOKEN_VERSION.name: TOKEN_VERSION
TOKEN_VERSION.comment: The version of the NFT token.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the NFT project. This field, along with metadata, will be filled in over time.

TOKENID.name: TOKENID
TOKENID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

NFT_COUNT.name: NFT_COUNT
NFT_COUNT.comment: The number of NFTs minted in this event.

TOTAL_PRICE.name: TOTAL_PRICE
TOTAL_PRICE.comment: The amount of the NFT event in the currency in which the transaction occurred.

TOTAL_PRICE_USD.name: TOTAL_PRICE_USD
TOTAL_PRICE_USD.comment: The amount of the NFT event in US dollars. This will be 0 for tokens without a decimal adjustment or hourly price.

CURRENCY_ADDRESS.name: CURRENCY_ADDRESS
CURRENCY_ADDRESS.comment: The token contract address for this NFT event. This will be `ETH` for native ETH transactions.

EZ_NFT_MINTS_ID.name: EZ_NFT_MINTS_ID
EZ_NFT_MINTS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This table contains a flattened easy version of the native transfers. This table uses the fact_transfers table as a base and filters down to only aptos tokens. The logic used to derive this table requires the withdrawal event to occur in the previous event to the deposit event and also requires the withdrawal event to be the same amount as the deposit event. The only exception to that rule is when a "CoinRegisterEvent" occurs in between the withdraw and deposit events. Any transfers that do not meet this criteria are not included in this table.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The account address that sent the transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The account address that received the transfer.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of a token. For example, if a token has 18 decimals, then the amount of 1 token is 10^18.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The full address of the token. This string contains the account,module, and resource.

EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_NFT_MINTS
metadata.comment: This table contains NFT mints events

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp at which the NFT event occurred.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number at which the NFT event occurred.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the NFT event. This is not necessarily unique in this table as a transaction may contain multiple NFT events.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The event number within a transaction

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of NFT event in this transaction, either sale, bid_won or redeem.

NFT_FROM_ADDRESS.name: NFT_FROM_ADDRESS
NFT_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of the NFT in the transaction.

NFT_TO_ADDRESS.name: NFT_TO_ADDRESS
NFT_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of the NFT in the transaction.

NFT_ADDRESS.name: NFT_ADDRESS
NFT_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address of the NFT.

TOKEN_VERSION.name: TOKEN_VERSION
TOKEN_VERSION.comment: The version of the NFT token.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the NFT project. This field, along with metadata, will be filled in over time.

TOKENID.name: TOKENID
TOKENID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

NFT_COUNT.name: NFT_COUNT
NFT_COUNT.comment: The number of NFTs minted in this event.

TOTAL_PRICE_RAW.name: TOTAL_PRICE_RAW
TOTAL_PRICE_RAW.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of the NFT event in the currency in which the transaction occurred.

CURRENCY_ADDRESS.name: CURRENCY_ADDRESS
CURRENCY_ADDRESS.comment: The token contract address for this NFT event. This will be `ETH` for native ETH transactions.

FACT_NFT_MINTS_ID.name: FACT_NFT_MINTS_ID
FACT_NFT_MINTS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A comprehensive fact table holding id and provider specific open, high, low, close hourly prices, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this fact based model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_prices_hourly.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

OPEN.name: OPEN
OPEN.comment: Opening price of the recorded hour in USD.

HIGH.name: HIGH
HIGH.comment: Highest price of the recorded hour in USD

LOW.name: LOW
LOW.comment: Lowest price of the recorded hour in USD

CLOSE.name: CLOSE
CLOSE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID
FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates bridge activity for bridge deposits and transfers sent from the following protocols: CELER, LAYER ZERO, MOVER, and WORMHOLE. Note, this table only includes records for the protocols listed above with live, onchain bridge activity and may not represent the complete bridging picture.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The platform or protocol from which the bridge transaction or event originates.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract responsible for handling the bridge deposit or transfer. This contract mediates the transfer and ensures that assets are sent and received appropriately.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The third segment of the event type

TX_SENDER.name: TX_SENDER
TX_SENDER.comment: Sender is the address of the originator account for a transaction. A transaction must be signed by the originator.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The address that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer. This address is the sender of the tokens/assets being bridged to the destination chain.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the bridged tokens on the target chain after the completion of the bridge transaction. For non-evm chains, the hex address is decoded/encoded to match the data format of the destination chain, where possible.

SOURCE_CHAIN.name: SOURCE_CHAIN
SOURCE_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain network from which the assets are being bridged.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain network to which the assets are being bridged.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address associated with the token that is being bridged. It provides a unique identifier for the token within its origin blockchain.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of a token. For example, if a token has 18 decimals, then the amount of 1 token is 10^18.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of a token.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: Unique identifier for the event. This is a monotonically increasing integer that is incremented for each event. This is useful for determining the order of changes.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_BLOCKS
metadata.comment:
This table contains "block" level data for the Aptos blockchain. This table can be used to analyze trends at a block level, for example total transactions over time.
"The Aptos blockchain doesn't have an explicit notion of a block — it only uses blocks for batching and executing transactions.
A transaction at height 0 is the first transaction (genesis transaction), and a transaction at height 100 is the 101st transaction in the transaction store."

BLOCK_HASH.name: BLOCK_HASH

FIRST_VERSION.name: FIRST_VERSION
FIRST_VERSION.comment: The version number of the first transaction in the block.

LAST_VERSION.name: LAST_VERSION
LAST_VERSION.comment: The version number of the last transaction in the block.

TX_COUNT.name: TX_COUNT
TX_COUNT.comment: The count of transactions in this block.

FACT_BLOCKS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for the Aptos blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash and version.
For more information see [aptos.dev docs] Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction. Values will be one of "block_metadata_transaction","state_checkpoint_transaction","user_transaction".

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: Sender is the address of the originator account for a transaction. A transaction must be signed by the originator.

SIGNATURE.name: SIGNATURE
SIGNATURE.comment: A signature is the result of hashing the signing message with the client's private key. By default Aptos uses the Ed25519 scheme to generate the signature of the raw transaction.

PAYLOAD.name: PAYLOAD
PAYLOAD.comment: The data that is being carried by a transaction.

PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.name: PAYLOAD_FUNCTION
PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.comment: The function that is being called in the transaction payload.

CHANGES.name: CHANGES
CHANGES.comment: The changes that the transaction executed.

EVENTS.name: EVENTS
EVENTS.comment: The events that the transaction executed.

GAS_UNIT_PRICE.name: GAS_UNIT_PRICE
GAS_UNIT_PRICE.comment: The cost per unit of gas, determining the transaction fee paid by the sender for each unit of computational resource consumed

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The amount of gas used for the transaction

MAX_GAS_AMOUNT.name: MAX_GAS_AMOUNT
MAX_GAS_AMOUNT.comment: The maximum amount of gas allocated for the execution of a transaction

EXPIRATION_TIMESTAMP_SECS.name: EXPIRATION_TIMESTAMP_SECS
EXPIRATION_TIMESTAMP_SECS.comment: The time at which the transaction ceases to valid.

VM_STATUS.name: VM_STATUS
VM_STATUS.comment: For failed transactions, this fields provides context to why the transaction failed. For successful transactions, this field will be set to `Executed successfully`.

ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.name: ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH
ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash of a Merkle accumulator.

EVENT_ROOT_HASH.name: EVENT_ROOT_HASH
EVENT_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash for the event.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programmatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets.

CREATOR.name: CREATOR
CREATOR.comment: Name of the label creator - for now, this will always be "Flipside."

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: Address unique to an individual wallet, validator, or token.

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE
LABEL_TYPE.comment: A broad category that describes what a label is representing.

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: Adds more detail to the label type.

LABEL.name: LABEL
LABEL.comment: The label or name of the address.

DIM_LABELS_ID.name: DIM_LABELS_ID
DIM_LABELS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION

CREATION_NUMBER.name: CREATION_NUMBER

SEQUENCE_NUMBER.name: SEQUENCE_NUMBER

metadata.name: EZ_NFT_SALES
metadata.comment: A convenience model that combines fact nft sales with tokens and prices

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp at which the NFT event occurred.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number at which the NFT event occurred.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the NFT event. This is not necessarily unique in this table as a transaction may contain multiple NFT events.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The event number within a transaction

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of NFT event in this transaction, either sale, bid_won or redeem.

BUYER_ADDRESS.name: BUYER_ADDRESS
BUYER_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the buyer of the NFT in the transaction.

NFT_ADDRESS.name: NFT_ADDRESS
NFT_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address of the NFT.

TOKEN_VERSION.name: TOKEN_VERSION
TOKEN_VERSION.comment: The version of the NFT token.

PLATFORM_ADDRESS.name: PLATFORM_ADDRESS
PLATFORM_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the exchange used for the transaction.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the NFT project. This field, along with metadata, will be filled in over time.

TOKENID.name: TOKENID
TOKENID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

PLATFORM_NAME.name: PLATFORM_NAME
PLATFORM_NAME.comment: The name of the exchange used for the trade.

PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION.name: PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION
PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION.comment: The version of the exchange contract used for the transaction.

TOTAL_PRICE.name: TOTAL_PRICE
TOTAL_PRICE.comment: The amount of the NFT event in the currency in which the transaction occurred.

TOTAL_PRICE_USD.name: TOTAL_PRICE_USD
TOTAL_PRICE_USD.comment: The amount of the NFT event in US dollars. This will be 0 for tokens without a decimal adjustment or hourly price.

PLATFORM_FEE.name: PLATFORM_FEE
PLATFORM_FEE.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of fees paid to the platform for this NFT event in the transaction's currency.

PLATFORM_FEE_USD.name: PLATFORM_FEE_USD
PLATFORM_FEE_USD.comment: The amount of fees paid to the platform for this NFT event in US dollars.

CREATOR_FEE.name: CREATOR_FEE
CREATOR_FEE.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of fees paid to the NFT collection as royalty payments for this NFT event in the transaction's currency.

CREATOR_FEE_USD.name: CREATOR_FEE_USD
CREATOR_FEE_USD.comment: The amount of fees paid to the NFT collection as royalty payments for this NFT event in US dollars.

TOTAL_FEES.name: TOTAL_FEES
TOTAL_FEES.comment: The total amount of fees paid relating to the NFT purchase in the transaction currency. This includes royalty payments to creators and platform fees. Please note, this does not include the gas fee.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The total amount of fees paid relating to the NFT purchase in US dollars. This includes royalty payments to creators and platform fees. Please note, this does not include the gas fee.

AGGREGATOR_NAME.name: AGGREGATOR_NAME
AGGREGATOR_NAME.comment: The name of the aggregator platform where the sale took place. If the sale did not take place via an aggregator platform, then the value will be null.

CURRENCY_ADDRESS.name: CURRENCY_ADDRESS
CURRENCY_ADDRESS.comment: The token contract address for this NFT event. This will be `ETH` for native ETH transactions.

EZ_NFT_SALES_ID.name: EZ_NFT_SALES_ID
EZ_NFT_SALES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This table contains Deposit and Withdraw events from the coin module as well as  Deposit, Withdraw, DepositEvent, and WithdrawEvent from the fungible_asset module on the aptos blockchain. Note: transfers with a 0 amount are excluded.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: Unique identifier for the event. This is a monotonically increasing integer that is incremented for each event. This is useful for determining the order of changes.

CREATION_NUMBER.name: CREATION_NUMBER
CREATION_NUMBER.comment: Ceation number corresponding to the event stream originating from the given account.

TRANSFER_EVENT.name: TRANSFER_EVENT
TRANSFER_EVENT.comment: The type of transfer event. Value will either be  'WithdrawEvent' or 'DepositEvent'

ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The top level address for this event.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of a token. For example, if a token has 18 decimals, then the amount of 1 token is 10^18.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The full address of the token. This string contains the account,module, and resource.

IS_FUNGIBLE.name: IS_FUNGIBLE
IS_FUNGIBLE.comment: Boolean indicating whether the transfer was conducted using the legacy coin transfer mechanism (a simpler, original method for transferring coins) or the fungible_asset module (a newer, more flexible system for managing fungible assets).

FACT_TRANSFERS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSFERS_ID
FACT_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_STATE_CHECKPOINT
metadata.comment: These transactions are appended at the end of the block and is used as a checkpoint milestone.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction. Values will be one of "block_metadata_transaction","state_checkpoint_transaction","user_transaction".

VM_STATUS.name: VM_STATUS
VM_STATUS.comment: For failed transactions, this fields provides context to why the transaction failed. For successful transactions, this field will be set to `Executed successfully`.

ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.name: ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH
ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash of a Merkle accumulator.

EVENT_ROOT_HASH.name: EVENT_ROOT_HASH
EVENT_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash for the event.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_STATE_CHECKPOINT_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_STATE_CHECKPOINT_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_STATE_CHECKPOINT_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_CHANGES
metadata.comment: This table contains the flattened changes from the transaction. Each change will have a unique change index within a transaction.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction. Values will be one of "block_metadata_transaction","state_checkpoint_transaction","user_transaction".

PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.name: PAYLOAD_FUNCTION
PAYLOAD_FUNCTION.comment: The function that is being called in the transaction payload.

CHANGE_INDEX.name: CHANGE_INDEX
CHANGE_INDEX.comment: Unique identifier for the change. This is a monotonically increasing integer that is incremented for each change. This is useful for determining the order of changes.

CHANGE_TYPE.name: CHANGE_TYPE
CHANGE_TYPE.comment: The "type" object from within this change. Values are: delete_resource, delete_table_item, write_module, write_resource, write_table_item.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: The top level address for this change.

HANDLE.name: HANDLE
HANDLE.comment: The top level handle for this change.

INNER_CHANGE_TYPE.name: INNER_CHANGE_TYPE
INNER_CHANGE_TYPE.comment: The full three-part descriptive change type from change. The inner change type consists of the change_address :: change_module :: change_resource.

CHANGE_ADDRESS.name: CHANGE_ADDRESS
CHANGE_ADDRESS.comment: The first segment of the inner change type

CHANGE_MODULE.name: CHANGE_MODULE
CHANGE_MODULE.comment: The second segment of the inner change type

CHANGE_RESOURCE.name: CHANGE_RESOURCE
CHANGE_RESOURCE.comment: The third segment of the inner change type

KEY.name: KEY
KEY.comment: The key value for the write_table_item change

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value for the write_table_item change

FACT_CHANGES_ID.name: FACT_CHANGES_ID
FACT_CHANGES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: Deprecating soon! Note, only the ID and DECIMALS columns are being removed. The dim_asset_metadata table itself is not being deprecated. 'A comprehensive dimensional table holding asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each id, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this dimensional model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_asset_metadata.'

DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_BLOCK_METADATA
metadata.comment: These transactions are inserted at the beginning of the block. A BlockMetadata transaction can also mark the end of an epoch and trigger reward distribution to validators.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction. Values will be one of "block_metadata_transaction","state_checkpoint_transaction","user_transaction".

EPOCH.name: EPOCH
EPOCH.comment: An epoch in the Aptos blockchain is defined as a duration of time, in seconds, during which a number of blocks are voted on by the validators, the validator set is updated, and the rewards are distributed to the validators. The Aptos mainnet epoch is set as 7200 seconds (two hours).

EVENTS.name: EVENTS
EVENTS.comment: The events that the transaction executed.

CHANGES.name: CHANGES
CHANGES.comment: The changes that the transaction executed.

ROUND.name: ROUND
ROUND.comment: A round number is a shared counter used to select leaders during an epoch of the consensus protocol.

VM_STATUS.name: VM_STATUS
VM_STATUS.comment: For failed transactions, this fields provides context to why the transaction failed. For successful transactions, this field will be set to `Executed successfully`.

ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.name: ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH
ACCUMULATOR_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash of a Merkle accumulator.

EVENT_ROOT_HASH.name: EVENT_ROOT_HASH
EVENT_ROOT_HASH.comment: The root hash for the event.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_BLOCK_METADATA_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_BLOCK_METADATA_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_BLOCK_METADATA_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis. Stats for the current hour will be updated as new data arrives.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.comment: The hour of the timestamp of the block.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN
BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.comment: The minimum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS
TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.comment: The number of successful transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED
TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.comment: The number of failed transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_SENDER_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_SENDER_COUNT
UNIQUE_SENDER_COUNT.comment: The number of unique sender address in the hour.

UNIQUE_PAYLOAD_FUNCTION_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_PAYLOAD_FUNCTION_COUNT
UNIQUE_PAYLOAD_FUNCTION_COUNT.comment: The number of unique payload functions in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.name: TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE
TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in the native fee currency.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID
EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table for determining token prices by address and blockchain, and native asset prices by symbol and blockchain. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one price per hour per unique asset and blockchain.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

IS_IMPUTED.name: IS_IMPUTED
IS_IMPUTED.comment: A flag indicating if the price was imputed, or derived, from the last arriving record. This is generally used for tokens with low-liquidity or inconsistent reporting.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID
EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: A convenience table that contains DEX swap activity for the following protocols: animeswap, auxexchange, batswap, cellana, cetus, hippo, liquidswap, pancake, sushi, thala, and tsunami. This table contains combines the raw on chain data with decimal adjustments, token symbols, or prices.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: Unique identifier for the event. This is a monotonically increasing integer that is incremented for each event. This is useful for determining the order of changes.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The name of the platform where the swap occurred.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The full address of the inbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

SYMBOL_IN.name: SYMBOL_IN
SYMBOL_IN.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

SYMBOL_OUT.name: SYMBOL_OUT
SYMBOL_OUT.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ
AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.comment: The non decimal adjusted amount of a token of the inbound token for the swap.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.comment: The non decimal adjusted amount of a token of the outbound token for the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: The full address of the outbound token. This string contains the account, module, and resource.

EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID
EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_NFT_SALES
metadata.comment: This table contains NFT sale events from bluemove, mercato, okx, seashrine,souffl3,topaz, and wapal

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp at which the NFT event occurred.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number at which the NFT event occurred.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the NFT event. This is not necessarily unique in this table as a transaction may contain multiple NFT events.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The event number within a transaction

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of NFT event in this transaction, either sale, bid_won or redeem.

BUYER_ADDRESS.name: BUYER_ADDRESS
BUYER_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the buyer of the NFT in the transaction.

NFT_ADDRESS.name: NFT_ADDRESS
NFT_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address of the NFT.

TOKEN_VERSION.name: TOKEN_VERSION
TOKEN_VERSION.comment: The version of the NFT token.

PLATFORM_ADDRESS.name: PLATFORM_ADDRESS
PLATFORM_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the exchange used for the transaction.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the NFT project. This field, along with metadata, will be filled in over time.

TOKENID.name: TOKENID
TOKENID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

PLATFORM_NAME.name: PLATFORM_NAME
PLATFORM_NAME.comment: The name of the exchange used for the trade.

PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION.name: PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION
PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION.comment: The version of the exchange contract used for the transaction.

TOTAL_PRICE_RAW.name: TOTAL_PRICE_RAW
TOTAL_PRICE_RAW.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of the NFT event in the currency in which the transaction occurred.

PLATFORM_FEE_RAW.name: PLATFORM_FEE_RAW
PLATFORM_FEE_RAW.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of fees paid to the platform for this NFT event in the transaction's currency.

CREATOR_FEE_RAW.name: CREATOR_FEE_RAW
CREATOR_FEE_RAW.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of fees paid to the NFT collection as royalty payments for this NFT event in the transaction's currency.

TOTAL_FEES_RAW.name: TOTAL_FEES_RAW
TOTAL_FEES_RAW.comment: The non-decimal adjusted total amount of fees paid relating to the NFT purchase in the transaction currency. This includes royalty payments to creators and platform fees. Please note, this does not include the gas fee.

AGGREGATOR_NAME.name: AGGREGATOR_NAME
AGGREGATOR_NAME.comment: The name of the aggregator platform where the sale took place. If the sale did not take place via an aggregator platform, then the value will be null.

CURRENCY_ADDRESS.name: CURRENCY_ADDRESS
CURRENCY_ADDRESS.comment: The token contract address for this NFT event. This will be `ETH` for native ETH transactions.

FACT_NFT_SALES_ID.name: FACT_NFT_SALES_ID
FACT_NFT_SALES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets.

INSERT_DATE.comment: The date the label was inserted in the table

ADDRESS.comment: Address that the label is for. This is the field that should be used to join other tables with labels.

CREATOR.comment: The name of the creator of the label

LABEL_TYPE.comment: A high-level category describing the addresses main function or ownership

LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: A sub-category nested within label type providing further detail

PROJECT_NAME.comment: Name of the controlling entity of the address
